content:
Case 2:
Initial presentation:
- Age: 24
- Sex: female
- Chief complaint: New skin changes on the back and extremities
- Vital signs: BP 120/75 mmHg, HR 116/min, T° 36.8 °C, respiratory rate 16/min.
Setting: Outpatient/Day Unit (Initial Presentation)
Capabilities: You are evaluating a clinically stable patient with new symptoms in an ambulatory or day-unit setting. You can perform a focused history and examination, obtain routine blood panels, and arrange standard non-invasive imaging as first-line tests. Invasive tissue sampling and specialized analyses usually require additional coordination and are not available in this round.
Provide either a request for a single information item or solve.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "focused physical examination, with specific attention to the skin lesions"}}

response.request: focused physical examination, with specific attention to the skin lesions

content:
History Physical:
On June 4, 2025, the patient presented with new skin changes, first noticed by her partner on the back and now also on the extremities. She reported intermittent headaches and mild nausea, as well as chronic joint pain and stiffness. Otherwise, she was in good general condition, afebrile, and showed no signs of skin or mucosal GvHD. A marked psychological stress reaction was noted.
Current medication: pantoprazole 40 mg 1-0-0, vitamin D 20,000 IE 1×/week, calcium 500 mg 1-0-0, posaconazole 300 mg 1×/d, ruxolitinib 10 mg 1-0-1, valaciclovir 500 mg 1-0-1, co-trimoxazole 960 mg orally once daily, three times per week (Monday, Wednesday, Friday), steroid cream p.r.n.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "complete blood count"}}

Cbc:
CBC: Hb 14.2 g/dL, WBC 6.8 ×10⁹/L, Plt 240 ×10⁹/L; MCV 89 fL, MCH 30 pg; differential: neutrophils 58%, lymphocytes 32%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 2%, basophils 1%.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "description of the skin lesions (morphology, distribution, evolution)"}}

response.request: description of the skin lesions (morphology, distribution, evolution)

content: 'history_physical' already provided.
{"response": {"action": "request", "request": "basic metabolic panel and liver function tests"}}

content:

Round 2 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward (Early Workup)
Capabilities: The patient is now admitted for further evaluation. You can organize targeted tissue sampling and a broader diagnostic workup, including advanced imaging and initial pathology assessments. Some specialized analyses may still be pending or require referral to external laboratories. They will be available in the next round.

content: {"response": {"action": "request", "request": "skin biopsy with pathology report"}}

Skin Biopsy:
Skin biopsy (2025-06-07): The dermis and subcutis are diffusely infiltrated by atypical hematopoietic cells in a perivascular and interstitial distribution. The cells show irregular nuclear contours, vesicular chromatin, and occasional nucleoli. Mitotic figures are frequent. Immunohistochemistry: CD34-, CD33+, MPO+, Lysozyme and CD68+, CD117+, negative for CD20-, CD19-, CD3-. Molecular pathology: NPM1 mutation present in cutaneous infiltration.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Leukemia cutis secondary to acute myeloid leukemia (NPM1-mutated)", "differentials": ["Myeloid sarcoma", "Chronic graft-versus-host disease with atypical infiltrate"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Arrange bone marrow aspirate and biopsy to assess extent of disease and hematologic remission status", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Initiate systemic anti-leukemic therapy as indicated by bone marrow and molecular findings (e.g., re-induction chemotherapy or targeted therapy for NPM1-mutated AML)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Multidisciplinary team involvement for assessment of further extramedullary disease (e.g., PET-CT)", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Leukemia cutis secondary to acute myeloid leukemia (NPM1-mutated)
differentials: Chronic graft-versus-host disease with atypical infiltrate

treatment_plan.action: Arrange bone marrow aspirate and biopsy to assess extent of disease and hematologic remission status
Initiate systemic anti-leukemic therapy as indicated by bone marrow and molecular findings (e.g., re-induction chemotherapy or targeted therapy for NPM1-mutated AML)
Multidisciplinary team involvement for assessment of further extramedullary disease (e.g., PET-CT)

Round 3 begins.
Setting: Inpatient Ward / Multidisciplinary Tumor Board (Definitive Characterization)
Capabilities: You now have access to results from specialized tests with longer turnaround times, including detailed pathology, molecular profiling, and measurable residual disease assessments, as well as a complete overview of prior therapies. You are expected to integrate these data into a definitive diagnosis, risk assessment, and treatment strategy.
Request additional information or solve.
Final round - provide definitive diagnosis and treatment plan.

content:
Mrd Status:
Flow cytrometry: MRD negative in bone marrow. NPM1 in bone marrow negative. Chimerism: <0.06% recipient.

{"response": {"action": "solve", "solve": {"working_diagnosis": "Isolated extramedullary relapse (leukemia cutis) of NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia with negative bone marrow and systemic MRD", "differentials": ["Myeloid sarcoma (granulocytic sarcoma)", "Reactive atypical cutaneous infiltrate"], "treatment_plan": [{"action": "Initiate systemic salvage therapy (e.g., targeted or cytotoxic chemotherapy suitable for NPM1-mutated AML, or consider hypomethylating agents)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 1}, {"action": "Consider local therapy to skin lesions (e.g., radiotherapy) if lesions are symptomatic or persistent despite systemic therapy", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 2}, {"action": "Refer for allogeneic stem cell transplant evaluation (if not already performed, or consider donor lymphocyte infusion/second transplant in relapsed post-transplant patient)", "intention": "therapeutic", "priority": 3}, {"action": "Continue multidisciplinary tumor board reviews and surveillance with MRD assessment to promptly identify systemic progression", "intention": "diagnostic", "priority": 3}]}}}

solve.working_diagnosis: Isolated extramedullary relapse (leukemia cutis) of NPM1-mutated acute myeloid leukemia with negative bone marrow and systemic MRD

treatment_plan.action: Initiate systemic salvage therapy (e.g., targeted or cytotoxic chemotherapy suitable for NPM1-mutated AML, or consider hypomethylating agents)
Consider local therapy to skin lesions (e.g., radiotherapy) if lesions are symptomatic or persistent despite systemic therapy
Refer for allogeneic stem cell transplant evaluation (if not already performed, or consider donor lymphocyte infusion/second transplant in relapsed post-transplant patient)
Continue multidisciplinary tumor board reviews and surveillance with MRD assessment to promptly identify systemic progression
